perturbation.original_text: III. Non-Disclosure Recipient must hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, de -compile, disassemble, or transfer, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Confidential Information or any portion of it. Recipient must ensure that the Confidential Information is maintained by it in confidence in a manner so as to e nsure that it will not be viewed or taken by any unauthorized person or further disclosed in a manner not authorized in this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement . Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B. As described above, you may exercise your right of access to Confidential Information only through individuals who: (i) have requested access to Confid ential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non Disclosure Agreement by providing a signed copy of App endix A or Appendix B to GTC . You agree to keep an updated list of such individuals and to ensure that only those individuals have access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: III. Non-Disclosure Recipient must hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, de -compile, disassemble, or transfer, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Confidential Information or any portion of it, except when providing expert testimony in legal proceedings. Recipient must ensure that the Confidential Information is maintained by it in confidence in a manner so as to e nsure that it will not be viewed or taken by any unauthorized person or further disclosed in a manner not authorized in this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement . Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B. As described above, you may exercise your right of access to Confidential Information only through individuals who: (i) have requested access to Confid ential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non Disclosure Agreement by providing a signed copy of App endix A or Appendix B to GTC . You agree to keep an updated list of such individuals and to ensure that only those individuals have access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the non-disclosure clause by adding "except when providing expert testimony in legal proceedings." The original text strictly prohibits any disclosure of Confidential Information. The added exception conflicts with this blanket prohibition, creating uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality obligations. Specifically, it contradicts the detailed list of exceptions given in points (a) and (b) which previously were the only permissible forms of disclosure. It also weakens the overall purpose of NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: III. Non-Disclosure Recipient must hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, de -compile, disassemble, or transfer, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Confidential Information or any portion of it, except when providing expert testimony in legal proceedings. Recipient must ensure that the Confidential Information is maintained by it in confidence in a manner so as to e nsure that it will not be viewed or taken by any unauthorized person or further disclosed in a manner not authorized in this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement . Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B. As described above, you may exercise your right of access to Confidential Information only through individuals who: (i) have requested access to Confid ential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non Disclosure Agreement by providing a signed copy of App endix A or Appendix B to GTC . You agree to keep an updated list of such individuals and to ensure that only those individuals have access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except when providing expert testimony in legal proceedings') to the strict non-disclosure obligation outlined in the original text. This exception directly contradicts the subsequent clauses (a) and (b), which specify the *sole* permissible disclosures and the procedure of Appendix A and B, and creates an inconsistency about when and to whom confidential information can be disclosed, thus the change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) contains the added exception, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XII. Other Provisions If any pa rt of the Agreement is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the rest of the Agreement will still remain effective. GTC may exercise its rights under this Agreement at any time, even if they delayed doing so or failed to do so in the past. All Confidentia l Information is provided “as is” and with all faults . GTC shall not be liable for the accuracy or completeness of Confidential Information. GTC is not responsible for Recipient’s use of Confidential Information . Recipient agrees to hold GTC harmless an d indemnify them for any and all damages and liabilities arising from any action you may take with respect to Confidential Information. Recipient acknowledge s that GTC is not responsible or liable for any other entity’s designation of information as CEII, including any improper designation.
perturbation.changed_text: XII. Other Provisions If any part of the Agreement is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the rest of the Agreement will still remain effective, unless the invalid or unenforceable part is deemed a material term, in which case the entire agreement is void. GTC may exercise its rights under this Agreement only within one year of the date of the alleged breach. All Confidential Information is provided “as is” and with all faults . GTC shall not be liable for the accuracy or completeness of Confidential Information. GTC is not responsible for Recipient’s use of Confidential Information . Recipient agrees to hold GTC harmless and indemnify them for any and all damages and liabilities arising from any action you may take with respect to Confidential Information. Recipient acknowledge s that GTC is not responsible or liable for any other entity’s designation of information as CEII, including any improper designation.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "unless the invalid or unenforceable part is deemed a material term, in which case the entire agreement is void" contradicts the initial statement that 'the rest of the Agreement will still remain effective.' It creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement if a term is deemed material and unenforceable. Additionally, the clause 'GTC may exercise its rights under this Agreement only within one year of the date of the alleged breach,' directly contradicts the previous clause allowing GTC to exercise its rights at any time, even if they delayed in the past.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XII. Other Provisions If any part of the Agreement is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the rest of the Agreement will still remain effective, unless the invalid or unenforceable part is deemed a material term, in which case the entire agreement is void. GTC may exercise its rights under this Agreement only within one year of the date of the alleged breach. All Confidential Information is provided “as is” and with all faults . GTC shall not be liable for the accuracy or completeness of Confidential Information. GTC is not responsible for Recipient’s use of Confidential Information . Recipient agrees to hold GTC harmless and indemnify them for any and all damages and liabilities arising from any action you may take with respect to Confidential Information. Recipient acknowledge s that GTC is not responsible or liable for any other entity’s designation of information as CEII, including any improper designation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces two contradictions. First, it adds 'unless the invalid or unenforceable part is deemed a material term, in which case the entire agreement is void,' which directly contradicts the original statement that 'the rest of the Agreement will still remain effective.' Second, it limits GTC's right to exercise its rights to 'only within one year of the date of the alleged breach,' contradicting the previous clause allowing GTC to exercise its rights 'at any time, even if they delayed doing so or failed to do so in the past.' These changes create clear and direct conflicts within the document as shown in Perturbation Explanation and adhere to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: IX. Injunctive Relief In the event of a breach or threatened breach or intended breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by Recipient , GTC, in addition to any other rights and remedies available to it at law o r
perturbation.changed_text: IX. Injunctive Relief In the event of a breach or threatened breach or intended breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by Recipient , GTC may seek monetary damages up to $1000. Injunctive relief is explicitly waived by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction by stating that injunctive relief is explicitly waived, while the original implied that injunctive relief was a valid remedy in the case of a breach. This fundamentally alters the remedies available to GTC and creates an in-text inconsistency that would be grounds for legal dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IX. Injunctive Relief In the event of a breach or threatened breach or intended breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by Recipient , GTC may seek monetary damages up to $1000. Injunctive relief is explicitly waived by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly waives injunctive relief, while the original text implies that it is available to GTC in the event of a breach. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, reinforcing the direct contradiction introduced by stating that injunctive relief is waived, directly conflicting with the original implication that it was a potential remedy. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of a contract providing conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: VI. Property Rights Confidential Information is and at all times remains the property of GTC. Confi dential Information disclosed in connection with any work performed for GTC may be used only as permitted by this Non-Disclosure Agreement, and GTC does not convey any proprietary right or license, whether implied or otherwise, except as may be expressly g ranted in this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: VI. Property Rights Confidential Information is and at all times remains the property of GTC, except for derivative information created by the Recipient. Confidential Information disclosed in connection with any work performed for GTC may be used only as permitted by this Non-Disclosure Agreement, and GTC does not convey any proprietary right or license, whether implied or otherwise, except as may be expressly granted in this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating "Confidential Information is and at all times remains the property of GTC, except for derivative information created by the Recipient." while at the same time the definition in Section I states that “Derivative Information” is also considered part of “Confidential Information”, now, the exception implies derivative information created by the recipient is not the property of GTC while it is still Confidential Information by definition, creating uncertainty regarding ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VI. Property Rights Confidential Information is and at all times remains the property of GTC, except for derivative information created by the Recipient. Confidential Information disclosed in connection with any work performed for GTC may be used only as permitted by this Non-Disclosure Agreement, and GTC does not convey any proprietary right or license, whether implied or otherwise, except as may be expressly granted in this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception for 'derivative information created by the Recipient' to the rule that Confidential Information is the property of GTC. However, according to the explanation, 'Derivative Information' is still considered part of 'Confidential Information', causing a direct conflict regarding ownership. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, further emphasizing the contradiction. Since the change makes the property rights unclear due to derivative information ownership, a strong contradiction exists.
